name: Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada
name_original: Cruces por la senda Viriato – circular desde Almendral de la Cañada
description: Hike the difficult 13.4-mile Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada, gaining 2663 feet with panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Almendral De La Cañada offers diverse trails through the northeastern foothills of the Sierra de San Vicente and the head of the Tiétar Valley, south of the Sierra de Gredos. The region features mid-mountain ecosystems, transitioning from lower grazing areas to higher elevations with mixed forests of oak and pine. Hikers can explore ancient routes like the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental, encounter centuries-old trees, and observe traditional dry stone walls and shepherd's hut foundations. This varied landscape provides a rich outdoor experience. Best hiking trails around Almendral De La Cañada The most popular hiking route is Ruta del Tiétar – circular from Iglesuela del Tiétar (PR-TO 20), a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Tiétar Valley landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabeza del Oso and Pelados Trail (PR-TO 21), a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. It features significant elevation changes through mountain ecosystems, offering views of the surrounding hills. Local hikers also love the Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with notable ascents, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Almendral De La Cañada is defined by mid-mountain ecosystems, ancient drovers' roads, and mixed forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Almendral De La Cañada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Almendral De La Cañada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Almendral De La Cañada, catering to various skill levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 36 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: Hiking in Almendral De La Cañada offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from lower grazing areas and dehesas to higher elevations with mixed forests of oak and pine. You'll traverse mid-mountain ecosystems, encounter centuries-old trees, and see traditional dry stone walls and the foundations of old shepherd's huts, reflecting the area's rich natural and pastoral heritage.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018292487/ruta-del-tietar-circular-from-iglesuela-del-tietar-pr-to-20" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Tiétar – circular from Iglesuela del Tiétar (PR-TO 20)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.4 km trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018243665/cerro-de-san-vicente-and-cabeza-del-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Vicente and Cabeza del Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.5 km long and also considered moderate.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Along the trails, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Hill (1320m)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2607538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass&lt;/a&gt; is another significant natural feature. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5450384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Justicia of Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt;, a historical monument.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: The trails in Almendral De La Cañada are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 35 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the historical elements like ancient drovers' roads, and the beautiful mixed forests.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018253970/senda-de-viriato-loop-from-almendral-de-la-canada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 21.5 km trail with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: Many trails in the region are suitable for dogs, especially those that follow ancient drovers' roads and traverse open dehesas. However, it's always recommended to check individual route details on komoot for specific restrictions or recommendations regarding pets, and to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almendral De La Cañada?
A: Almendral de la Cañada's climate and location make it suitable for hiking year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while winter can provide crisp air and unique scenery. Even summer hiking is possible, especially in the cooler mornings or evenings, taking advantage of the shaded forest sections.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Almendral De La Cañada has 14 easy routes perfect for families or beginners. These trails typically have less elevation gain and are shorter in distance, allowing for a relaxed outdoor experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical routes or paths to explore?
A: The area is rich in history, featuring ancient routes like the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental. This historic drovers' road, established in the 13th century, was used for transhumance and now offers hikers a glimpse into centuries of tradition as they share these paths with active tourism.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems, from dehesas to mixed forests, provide habitats for various wildlife. You might spot small mammals, birds, and reptiles, often finding shelter within the dry stone walls that dot the landscape. The ancient trees also support a rich biodiversity.
tips: Another section of the marked path Cabeza del Oso y Pelados (PR-TO 21) that runs along the border of Monte Peladas and Cabeza del Turmal, offering wonderful views of the Sierra de Gredos area.

The summit of Cruces at 1,373 meters above sea level is one of the most impressive panoramic points in the Navamorcuende region. It offers beautiful views in all directions so I recommend taking some time and enjoying the beautiful views while having a picnic up here to recharge your batteries.
Strategically located and oriented towards a clearing in the mountains, the bench offers beautiful views towards the Almendral de la Cañada while you take advantage of recovering energy.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada?
faqs.answer: This loop trail is approximately 21.5 kilometers (13.4 miles) long and typically takes around 6 hours and 40 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, breaks, and fitness level.

faqs.question: Is the Senda de Viriato Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its length of over 21 kilometers and an elevation gain of more than 800 meters, this trail is rated as difficult. While it offers rewarding scenery, it involves prolonged uphill sections and requires a good level of physical fitness and hiking experience. Beginners might find it challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Almendral de la Cañada. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for the loop. Specific trailhead directions can often be found on local signage or detailed hiking maps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring, autumn, and winter are generally recommended for hiking this route due to more comfortable temperatures. While suitable year-round, summer can be very hot in this region, so plan accordingly with plenty of water and early starts if hiking during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Senda de Viriato Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas with livestock or near wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike in the Sierra de San Vicente?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits required or entrance fees to access and hike this trail within the Sierra de San Vicente. The area is open for public enjoyment, but always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Senda de Viriato Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including cattle trails, forest tracks, and easy-to-walk footpaths. You'll encounter both prolonged uphill slopes, particularly on ascents towards viewpoints, and gradual descents. The path winds through dehesas, oak forests, and reforested pine forests.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll discover several notable spots, including a Trail through Monte Peladas, the Sierra Cruces summit, and The Viriato bench, which offers panoramic views of the Tiétar River valley and the Sierra de Gredos. The trail also passes by ancient stone walls, an 18th-19th century watering trough, and remnants of old shepherd's huts.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Senda de Viriato (GR 63), Mirador de Almendral de la Cañada, Cabeza del Oso y Pelados, and Sendero del Caño de la Cruz, among others, as you complete the loop.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat in Almendral de la Cañada after the hike?
faqs.answer: Almendral de la Cañada is a small village, and while options might be limited, you can usually find local establishments to grab a drink or a meal after your hike. It's a good idea to check local opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: What unique historical or natural features can I observe on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail follows ancient cattle trails (Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental), showcasing the region's historical transhumance practices. You'll also see centenary trees like oaks, chestnuts, and holm oaks, and the stone walls provide habitat for local wildlife. The area is steeped in the legacy of Viriato, a Lusitanian warrior, adding a cultural layer to the natural beauty.
